preamble: The retraction of [kʰ] to [q] is a phonetic shift where a voiceless aspirated velar stop moves back to a uvular position. This often occurs in the environment of back vowels or as a systemic shift in languages with uvular phonemes.

examples.note: Historical retraction of velars in specific back-vowel environments in some dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
